title: Feathered Punk Rockers Win Big Photo Prize!
summary: Imagine three tiny baby birds with spiky feathers that look exactly like punk-rock mohawks. That's the grand-prize-winning photo in the Smithsonian Magazine's 23rd annual photography contest! Photographer Jacqueline Burke, who is a veterinarian (an animal doctor), took the picture at a bird breeding ground in New Jersey. She called it "The Punk Rockers." The baby egrets (a type of white bird) were no bigger than a golf ball when curled up. They spent all their time squabbling—pecking and fighting with each other. Their long-legged parent stood nearby, watching. More than 17,000 photos from 108 countries were entered. Other winners include a barefoot woman at a Hindu festival, a teenager on a horse during a Navajo ceremony, and a colorful chameleon. Burke used to watch birds with binoculars, but in 2014 she switched to a camera. "We need to save species like this and save moments like this," she says. The photos make you want to look closer and discover amazing stories from around the world.
why_it_matters: These photos show you that amazing moments are happening everywhere—even in your own backyard—if you just pay attention and look closely.

A magazine about science and history that runs a photo contest
The photo that won first place in the contest

background_read: Smithsonian Magazine is a famous magazine that writes about science, history, and nature.
Photo contests like this one help people see amazing animals and places they might never visit.
The photographer is also a veterinarian, which shows her special connection to animals.
Article_Structure: WHO: Jacqueline Burke, a veterinarian who took the photo.
WHAT: A photo of baby egrets with punk-rock mohawks won the grand prize in a Smithsonian contest.
WHERE: The photo was taken at a bird breeding ground in New Jersey.
WHY: Burke wants people to appreciate wildlife and work to protect species like these.

perspectives.description: The contest celebrates amazing photography from around the world and highlights the beauty of nature.
Some people might think focusing on cute animals ignores bigger environmental problems.
The photo contest brings attention to different cultures and wildlife, encouraging people to explore more.
